vulnerability.reference: Teams overinterpret model-proxy improvements as genuine molecular quality, neglecting wet-lab validation.
vulnerability.generated: Over-reliance on proxy property models without experimental validation leaves teams unable to detect synthetically inaccessible compounds.

impact.reference: Late-stage failures from poor pharmacokinetics or toxicity, escalating costs and prolonging development timelines.
impact.generated: Resources are wasted on chemically invalid candidates, delaying or derailing drug discovery efforts.

dose_response.reference: Goal-aware fragment assembly can generate molecules that appear promising but are chemically invalid, and when drug discovery teams prioritize these proxy-optimized compounds without wet-lab validation, it causes late-stage failures from poor pharmacokinetics or toxicity.
dose_response.generated: Goal-aware fragment assembly with dynamic vocabulary optimization, through drug discovery teams prioritizing in silico property predictions, triggers over-reliance on proxy models that misses synthetically inaccessible compounds, leading to wasted resources on chemically invalid candidates and delayed drug discovery.
